heading: Max Incoming Connections
body:
Atlas sets the [limits for concurrent incoming connections](manual/reference/configuration-options) based on the cluster tier and
[class](storage-class-ui). Atlas connection limits apply per node.
For [sharded clusters](manual/sharding), Atlas connection
limits apply per [mongos](manual/core/sharded-cluster-query-router)
router. The number of [mongos](manual/core/sharded-cluster-query-router)
routers is equal to the number of replica set nodes across all shards.

Your [read preference](manual/core/read-preference) also contributes
to the total number of connections Atlas can allocate for a given query.

> **Example:** Your `M10` cluster has three nodes with a 1500 connection limit
> per node. Atlas reserves 10 connections per node. If you set
> your [read preference](manual/core/read-preference) to [secondary](manual/core/read-preference), Atlas
> can read from the two secondary nodes for a combined 2980 connection limit.

To learn more about connection limits and increasing your limits,
see [Connection Limits and Cluster Tier](connection-limits).
